description: FANOS services & transport known-answer tests: L4 storage addressing, CALYPSO self-certifying addresses, and PROTEUS epoch shaping (spec §L4, Part XII, §13). Any implementation must reproduce these exactly to interoperate. Pinned by the Rust reference test suite.

storage_addressing.comment: The L4 storage address of a key: digest = BLAKE3-keyed(H_label, key) with label 'FANOS-v1/storage'; the responsible node is MapToPoint(label, key) over the deployment field. Digest is lower-case hex; point is the canonical projective coordinate over GF(7).
storage_addressing.label: FANOS-v1/storage

calypso_address.comment: CALYPSO service addresses are now ONOMA addresses (post-quantum commitment, bech32m, version-agile) — strictly superseding the earlier base32(BLAKE3(pubkey)) scheme. The canonical address / mnemonic / per-epoch derivation known-answer vectors live in conformance/vectors/names.json (pinned by fanos-onoma/tests/conformance.rs).

proteus_shape.comment: The PROTEUS per-epoch transport shape is derived from a community secret and epoch. junk_len = junk_count * junk_size. All values are deterministic; the wire signature rotates every epoch. scramble_seed_prefix is the first 4 bytes of the 32-byte XOF-derived scramble seed.

health_view_gossip.comment: The DIAKRISIS health-view gossip (DiagGossip frame body) is 7 little-endian u16 values: age-in-milliseconds since the sender last directly observed each Fano point (0 = self / just seen, 65535 = never/stale). A peer is live iff some witness reports it within the liveness window (spec §6.4).
